_comment: Maps each voxel-distribution file in data/configs/voxeldistributions to a friendly label. Most Cubic Odyssey planets use procedurally-rolled biomes (m_biome0..7 == 'random'), so a strict 1-to-1 planet -> distribution table isn't recoverable from data. Instead we describe each distribution as a planet TYPE. The 'planets' list is best-effort: filled in where m_biome* hard-codes a recognisable biome set.

earth_voxels.label: Earth-like worlds
earth_voxels.description: Temperate terrestrial planets: deserts, plains, mountains, hills, and lakes. The classic mining playground.

barren_voxels.label: Barren worlds
barren_voxels.description: Rocky, low-vegetation planets with sparse atmosphere. Mid-tier ore rich.

crystalline_voxels.label: Crystalline worlds
crystalline_voxels.description: Worlds with glass and crystal terrain. Gem-bearing layers.

oceanic_voxels.label: Oceanic worlds
oceanic_voxels.description: Water-dominated planets with sea-floor mineral deposits.

scorched_voxels.label: Scorched worlds
scorched_voxels.description: Hot, volcanic, lava-flow planets. Rare-matter rich.

shroomy_voxels.label: Shroomy worlds
shroomy_voxels.description: Fungal-biome planets dominated by mushrooms and damp terrain.

slimy_voxels.label: Slimy worlds
slimy_voxels.description: Gel / slime planets with reactive surface terrain.

xeno_voxels.label: Xeno worlds
xeno_voxels.description: Alien terrain — exotic biomes outside the normal classification.

majestic_voxels.label: Majestic worlds
majestic_voxels.description: Rare scenic worlds with unusual ore mixes.

default_deposits.label: Default deposits
default_deposits.description: Fallback ore distribution used when a planet does not declare its own.

debug.label: Debug
debug.description: Developer / debug distribution. Not seen in normal gameplay.
